name: Fresh Pineapple Upside-Down Cake
recipeIngredient: -1/2 inch thick slices, core removed (about 1/2 medium pineapple)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together granulated sugar and 1/2 cup butter in a mixer bowl at medium speed until smooth.
2. Lower the speed; add vanilla, nutmeg, eggs, yolks, and milk. Mix until all ingredients are incorporated.
3. Add baking powder and salt, then flour and cornmeal. Mix until the batter is fluffy.
4. Set aside the batter.
5. In a heavy, 10-inch oven-proof skillet over medium heat, combine the remaining 1/4 cup butter, Madeira, and pineapple slices.
6. Cook for 20 minutes, allowing the pineapple to release liquid. Remove the pineapple with a slotted spoon.
7. Reduce the liquid until it nearly becomes a glaze. Add brown sugar and cook for about 2 minutes until the mixture is smooth.
8. Replace the pineapple slices in the syrup and arrange them nicely.
9. Pour the cake batter over the fruit.
10. Bake at 375°F on the middle rack for 20 to 25 minutes. The cake is done when a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean.
11. Place a large platter over the skillet and turn it upside down to remove the cake from the skillet. Let the cake cool.
